design: paired: System A (schema only) vs System B (schema+invariant) on identical seeded F3 samples

interpretation.paired: Of 20 discordant pairs, schema-only alone was right on 0 and schema+invariant alone on 20. Only these discordant pairs carry information about a difference. McNemar (exact) p = 0.0000, so the difference favouring schema+invariant is significant at 0.05. Concordant pairs (both right or both wrong) are correctly ignored.
interpretation.system_a: Observed rate 40/80 = 50.0%. 95% Wilson CI [39.3%, 60.7%]: with this much data the true rate is consistent with anything in that band. Two rates whose intervals overlap are NOT distinguishable at this sample size — collect more samples to narrow the band.
interpretation.system_b: Observed rate 60/80 = 75.0%. 95% Wilson CI [64.5%, 83.2%]: with this much data the true rate is consistent with anything in that band. Two rates whose intervals overlap are NOT distinguishable at this sample size — collect more samples to narrow the band.
